CHS Alumni Weekly Newsletter

Season 2025-26 | Week 19 (Mar 2 to Mar 8)
Matchup Summaries Report Cards Betting Lines Player of the Week Fun Facts What If? Power Rankings Stats Corner Around the NBA Rumor Mill Record Book

Matchup Summaries

Luka my Balls 1776.20 def. Big Nik Energy 1333.85

Luka my Balls (15-4): 47 games, 37.79 fppg (G: 41.84 | F: 37.67 | C: 31.05)

Big Nik Energy (4-15): 39 games, 34.20 fppg (G: 36.35 | F: 27.10 | C: 42.06)

Season series: Nick 6-1 | All-time: Nick leads 37-27

Nick's coronation tour continued with his most dominant performance of the season, a 442.35-point demolition of Hayden that was never competitive from the opening tip. The -1365 moneyline and -414.5 spread from last week's betting lines turned out to be generous -- Nick covered by nearly 30 points. Luka Doncic (225.55 FP over 4 games) led the charge with a 79.75 FP explosion vs IND on March 6, while Karl-Anthony Towns (170.45 FP over 4 games) and Anthony Edwards (161.45 FP over 3 games) formed a three-headed monster that overwhelmed Hayden at every turn. Nick's guards outscored Hayden's by 302.50 FP, a staggering positional edge that rendered everything else academic.

Hayden's roster was gutted by 10 games lost to injury, continuing a season-long nightmare that has now reached 167 non-IL games lost -- the league's worst burden at 26.6% total. LaMelo Ball (165.60 FP over 4 games), Paolo Banchero (158.10 FP over 4 games), and a returning Devin Booker (150.70 FP over 4 games) did their best to keep things respectable, and Hayden's 42.06 FPPG at center actually won the positional battle, but the forward slot was a disaster at 27.10 FPPG with so many key pieces sidelined. Credit to Hayden for leaving zero games on the bench and committing no blunders -- there simply weren't enough healthy bodies to compete.

Nick has now won six straight against Hayden this season and extends the all-time series to 37-27. With the title already clinched and a 5-game cushion atop the standings at 15-4, this is a victory lap -- but Nick isn't coasting, posting the league's highest score this week with a 101.6% efficiency rating that says his healthy players are actually outperforming their projections.

Saboner 1559.25 def. Smaxey 1341.40

Smaxey (10-9): 36 games, 37.26 fppg (G: 37.90 | F: 39.18 | C: 32.61)

Saboner (9-10): 43 games, 36.26 fppg (G: 36.80 | F: 33.12 | C: 37.57)

Season series: Benton 4-3 | All-time: Garrett leads 33-31

Garrett's late-season surge is no longer a fluke -- it's a full-blown movement. The 217.85-point win over Benton extends Garrett's win streak to a season-best 4 games (Weeks 16--19), and he's now chasing his personal all-time record of 7 consecutive wins set during the 2021-22 season. Victor Wembanyama (241.65 FP over 4 games) was transcendent, posting a 78.95 FP masterpiece vs DET on March 5, while Tyler Herro (171.10 FP over 4 games) and Jalen Brunson (155.85 FP over 4 games) provided the kind of consistent secondary scoring that wins weeks. Garrett's guard corps dominated with 772.80 FP across 21 games, outpacing Benton's guards by 280.05 FP.

Benton entered as the -115 moneyline favorite with a -23.0 spread but never got traction. Six games lost to injury scattered across five different players disrupted lineup continuity, and the damage was compounded by 2 blunders worth 70.10 FP -- James Harden left on the bench with an open guard slot when Donovan Mitchell was out on March 3, and Trey Murphy III riding the pine with an open forward slot on March 8 while LeBron James sat. Desmond Bane (139.60 FP over 4 games) and Trey Murphy III (130.80 FP over 3 games) anchored a forward group that actually won the positional battle at 39.18 FPPG to Garrett's 33.12, but Benton's center production cratered at 32.61 FPPG with just 8 games played at the position. Only 36 total games played out of 47 scheduled tells the story of a week that got away.

With this result, Garrett pulls within a single game of Benton in the standings at 9-10 vs 10-9, flipping the all-time series lead to 33-31 in Garrett's favor. After beating all four league members in consecutive weeks (Benton in Week 16, Nick in Week 17, Hayden in Week 18, and now Benton again), Garrett is running hot -- averaging 1684.40 FP over his last 3 weeks, 12% above his season average.

Report Cards

Nick (Luka my Balls) B

Record: 15-4 | Won vs Hayden by 442.35 | 1776.20 FP | +1.6% vs projection | Injuries: 2 | Bench: 1 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: 4 adds (409.10 FP over 12 games, 34.09 fppg, 102.30 FP per add)

Nick posted the league's highest score for the week at 1776.20 FP and did it with clean management -- zero blunders, just 1 game left on the bench, and only 2 games lost to injury. The +1.6% efficiency rating means his players slightly outperformed projections across the board, a testament to strong roster construction even without Anthony Davis, who remains on the shelf with his hand injury. The waiver wire was a weapon this week: 4 adds generated 409.10 FP at 34.09 FPPG, headlined by OG Anunoby (149.55 FP over 4 games) and Jalen Suggs (142.50 FP over 4 games). The only blemish was Payton Pritchard's -3.7 FP stinker vs CHA on March 4, which set a new season-low single-game record, but it barely registered in a week this dominant. Nick's 20.1% season injury burden remains the league's lowest, and that floor of health has been the foundation of a 6th championship run.

Hayden (Big Nik Energy) C+

Record: 4-15 | Lost vs Nick by 442.35 | 1333.85 FP | -4.5% vs projection | Injuries: 10 | Bench: 0 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: 2 adds (227.75 FP over 6 games, 37.96 fppg, 113.90 FP per add)

A C+ for a 442-point loss might seem generous, but Hayden did everything within his control this week. Zero blunders, zero games left on the bench, and his 2 waiver adds generated 227.75 FP at an impressive 37.96 FPPG -- the best per-add rate in the league this week at 113.90 FP per add. The problem, as it has been all season, was health: 10 games lost to injury gutted the roster, and the -4.5% efficiency gap means even the players who did suit up underperformed their projections. The 26.6% season injury burden -- the league's worst, with 167 non-IL games lost -- continues to define Hayden's 2025-26 story. Locked into 4th place at 4-15, there's nothing left to play for except pride, but Hayden's roster management has been mostly blameless in what amounts to a season derailed by forces outside his control.

Garrett (Saboner) C

Record: 9-10 | Won vs Benton by 217.85 | 1559.25 FP | -2.6% vs projection | Injuries: 4 | Bench: 3 | Blunders: 0 | Waivers: 1 add (66.90 FP over 2 games, 33.45 fppg, 66.90 FP per add)

Four straight wins is four straight wins, but the C reflects a week where Garrett was carried by his stars rather than elevated by sharp management. The -2.6% efficiency rating means healthy players slightly underperformed projections, and 3 games were left on the bench -- including Kawhi Leonard's 45.00 FP day on March 6 that sat behind Jamal Murray's 18.75 FP in the UTIL slot. No blunders technically, since no starter slots were available for those bench players, but the missed optimization cost 46.45 potential points. Wemby's 241.65 FP accounted for 15.5% of the team's total output, a massive concentration of production that papered over thin depth. Still, results matter, and Garrett is now within 1 game of Benton for 2nd place at 9-10 -- a position that seemed unthinkable during the early-season struggles.

Benton (Smaxey) D+

Record: 10-9 | Lost vs Garrett by 217.85 | 1341.40 FP | -2.0% vs projection | Injuries: 6 | Bench: 5 | Blunders: 2 (70.10 FP wasted) | Waivers: 0 adds (0.00 FP over 0 games, 0.00 fppg, 0.00 FP per add)

The blunder problem that nearly cost Benton a shot at the title in Week 18 reared its head again. Two blunders totaling 70.10 FP wasted -- James Harden on the bench with an open guard slot on March 3 while Donovan Mitchell sat, and Trey Murphy III riding the pine on March 8 while LeBron James's forward slot was empty -- represent the kind of avoidable negligence that turns tight matchups into blowouts. Zero waiver adds in a week where 6 games were lost to injury is hard to defend, and 5 games left on the bench overall suggest a manager who wasn't paying attention when it mattered most. The 1341.40 FP total was the week's second-lowest, and the -2.0% efficiency gap compounds the narrative of a team that didn't get the most out of what it had. With Garrett now just 1 game back, Benton's grip on 2nd place is slipping -- and the blunder habit is the reason why.

Betting Lines

Luka my Balls vs Saboner
Team Spread Total Moneyline Win Prob Avg Score
Luka my Balls -269.5 o3530 -470 82.4% 1887.96
Saboner +269.5 u3530 +470 17.6% 1645.36

Nick enters as the heavy -470 favorite, and the 269.5-point spread reflects a sizable roster advantage even without Anthony Davis, who's still a couple of weeks away from returning from his hand injury. The headliner matchup pits Luka Doncic (55.63 projected FPPG) against Victor Wembanyama (54.60 projected FPPG), two franchise centerpieces separated by a single fantasy point per game in projections. Nick holds the guard edge (+38.14 FPPG), but Garrett actually has advantages at both forward (+27.66 FPPG) and center (+35.06 FPPG) -- a positional split that could make this more competitive than the moneyline suggests. Garrett's 4-game win streak and hot scoring trend (12% above his season average over the last 3 weeks) gives the +470 underdog real live upset potential, especially if Wemby delivers another 60-point week. Domantas Sabonis and Nikola Vucevic are both done for the season, and Stephen Curry remains out with his knee injury, so Garrett will need his remaining core to overperform. The season series sits at 5-1 in Nick's favor, and a Garrett loss would put him 7 games back of Nick.

Big Nik Energy vs Smaxey
Team Spread Total Moneyline Win Prob Avg Score
Big Nik Energy +305.0 o3500 +610 14.1% 1614.63
Smaxey -305.0 u3500 -610 85.9% 1889.31

The 305-point spread is the week's largest, and the -610 moneyline makes Benton a prohibitive favorite despite his recent struggles. The star power matchup features Nikola Jokic (59.00 projected FPPG), who's been the league's most dominant per-game player all season, going up against Tyrese Maxey (45.79 projected FPPG), who could return from his finger injury midweek to give Benton a massive boost. Benton sweeps the guard (+45.03 FPPG) and forward (+140.07 FPPG) positional matchups -- the forward gap is staggering -- while Hayden's center advantage (+60.01 FPPG) is built almost entirely on Jokic's shoulders. Benton has won 3 straight in the head-to-head, and this is essentially a must-win to maintain his hold on 2nd place with Garrett breathing down his neck just 1 game back. Hayden has Jalen Williams and Lauri Markkanen still sidelined, though VJ Edgecombe could return midweek from his back injury. On Benton's side, both Maxey and Jarrett Allen are expected back from their respective injuries, which could swing this from a competitive matchup into the rout the line implies.

Player of the Week

Victor Wembanyama

241.65 FP over 4 games (60.41 FPPG) | +10.6% vs projection | 15.5% of team output

Wembanyama is playing a different sport than everyone else right now. His 241.65 FP across 4 games was the week's highest individual output, anchored by a 78.95 FP eruption vs DET on March 5 that ranks among the top single-game performances of the entire season. The 7-foot-4 generational talent posted a 60.41 FPPG clip -- the only player in the league to average north of 60 this week -- and he did it while accounting for more than a sixth of Garrett's total team production. This is Wemby's second POTW of the season (his first came back in Week 1), and the bookend symmetry is poetic: the player who opened the season as the league's most electric performer is closing it the same way, carrying Garrett through a 4-game win streak that has reshaped the standings. With Domantas Sabonis and Nikola Vucevic done for the year and Stephen Curry still sidelined, Wembanyama has shouldered an enormous burden and answered it with arguably his best stretch of the season. POTW by manager this season: Hayden 6, Nick 5, Benton 5, Garrett 3.

Honorable Mentions: Luka Doncic posted 225.55 FP across 4 games (56.39 FPPG), including a 79.75 FP game vs IND on March 6 that was actually the week's single-highest scoring performance -- Luka narrowly lost the POTW nod on overall team impact. Tyler Herro delivered 171.10 FP across 4 games (42.78 FPPG), highlighted by a 77.75 FP outburst at CHA on March 6 that helped power Garrett's 4th consecutive victory.

Fun Facts

  • Garrett is just 2 wins behind Benton for #3 on the all-time wins leaderboard -- and with a 4-game win streak in his back pocket, that gap could vanish before the season ends.
  • Garrett has won 4 straight matchups, his longest win streak of the season (Weeks 16--19) -- and he's chasing his personal all-time record of 7 consecutive wins from the 2021-22 season.
  • Nick's guards outscored Hayden's by 302 FP this week -- one of the most lopsided positional advantages of the entire season.
  • Garrett's guards outscored Benton's by 280 FP this week, making the guard position the decisive battleground in both matchups.
  • Benton's forwards outscored Garrett's by 290 FP this week -- the lone positional battle Benton won, though it wasn't nearly enough to offset the guard deficit.
  • Hayden has lost 285 total injury games this season -- 68 more than Nick's league-low 217, a gap that tells the story of two very different seasons from the same starting line.
  • Nick's 61.1% career win rate leads the league by 9.3 percentage points over Hayden -- a lifetime of dominance that this 6th championship only cements further.

What If?

Benton committed 2 blunders this week, wasting 70.10 FP that never needed to leave the table. On March 3, James Harden (25.35 FP) sat on the bench while Donovan Mitchell's guard slot went empty -- a free 25 points Benton simply didn't collect. Then on March 8, Trey Murphy III (44.70 FP) rode the pine while LeBron James's forward slot was wide open. That's two days where a starter slot had no one playing and a healthy bench player was right there, ready to go. Combined with the optimal swap of Bam Adebayo (57.20 FP) over Jarrett Allen (8.85 FP) at center on March 3 and James Harden (42.05 FP) over Derrick White (21.95 FP) on March 8, Benton left a total of 138.50 points on the bench -- the week's worst lineup management by a wide margin.

Garrett left 46.45 points on the table but committed zero blunders. His biggest missed opportunity was Kawhi Leonard (45.00 FP) sitting behind Jamal Murray (18.75 FP) in the UTIL slot on March 6 -- a 26.25-point swing -- followed by Cooper Flagg (41.60 FP) on the bench while Josh Hart (21.40 FP) started at forward on March 8.

Nick and Hayden both managed clean lineups with zero blunders. Nick left just 22.10 FP on the bench with no meaningful swaps available. Hayden left nothing -- every available player was in the lineup.

The good news for both losers: even with perfect lineups, neither matchup would have flipped. Benton's optimal total still falls short of Garrett, and Hayden's margin was an impossible 442.35 points. But for Benton, who now has blunders in consecutive weeks, the pattern is becoming a liability -- setting your lineup isn't optional, especially with the race for 2nd place tightening by the week.

Power Rankings

Power Rankings

Rank Team Record Title Odds Trend
1 Luka my Balls 15-4 100.0% --
2 Smaxey 10-9 0.0% --
3 Saboner 9-10 0.0% --
4 Big Nik Energy 4-15 0.0% --

Nick's 6th championship has been locked in since Week 18, and the 15-4 record -- with a projected finish of 16.1-4.9 -- represents the kind of wire-to-wire dominance that has defined his career 61.1% win rate, the league's best by a mile. The only remaining drama at the top is whether he can match last season's 19-4 masterpiece. With Anthony Davis still a couple of weeks away from returning and Luka Doncic humming at 54.72 FPPG on the season, this roster has the ceiling to finish strong regardless.

The real action is the battle for 2nd, and it's tightening fast. Benton holds a 1-game lead at 10-9 with a 93.64% probability of finishing 2nd, but Garrett's 4-game win streak has turned what felt like a comfortable margin into a nail-biter. Benton projects to finish 11.0-10.0 while Garrett tracks toward 10.1-10.9 -- essentially a coin flip on paper. Benton enters Week 20 at 94.9% health with Tyrese Maxey and Jarrett Allen both expected back from their injuries, which should restore the firepower advantage. But Garrett is running hot at 12% above his season average over the last 3 weeks, riding Wembanyama and a suddenly deep guard rotation that has won him four straight.

Hayden is locked into 4th at 100.0% probability, and the 4-15 record is among the most painful in league history when you consider the roster talent. The luck index tells the story: at -4.8, Hayden is this season's unluckiest manager by a significant margin, with a scoring profile that suggests an 8.8-10.2 record rather than the 4-15 reality. The 26.6% season injury burden -- 285 total injury games, 68 more than any other team -- has been the defining storyline of Hayden's year. Looking ahead to Week 20, Jalen Williams should be back soon from his hamstring injury, and VJ Edgecombe could return midweek, but with Tyrese Haliburton and Lauri Markkanen still out, the health picture remains cloudy at 80.2%.

Garrett's surge from league doormat to legitimate contender for 2nd place is the season's best late-season story. At 9-10 with a -0.3 luck index, Garrett's record essentially matches his expected output -- this isn't a fluke built on schedule luck. It's a team carried by Victor Wembanyama (54.60 projected FPPG rest of season) despite losing Domantas Sabonis, Nikola Vucevic, and Stephen Curry to long-term injuries. The 24.5% season injury burden hasn't slowed the momentum, and with the all-time series against Benton now flipped to 33-31 in his favor, Garrett is writing a new chapter in a career that's still searching for its first championship.

Player NBA Team Position Proj FPPG Games This Week Games Next Week
Nickeil Alexander-Walker ATL PG,SG,SF 33.90 3 4
Jrue Holiday POR PG,SG 33.42 3 4
Reed Sheppard HOU PG,SG 32.90 3 4
Kyle Filipowski UTA PF,C 31.99 4 3
Immanuel Quickley TOR PG,SG 31.69 4 3

Payton Pritchard's -3.70 FP vs CHA on March 4 sets a new season-low single-game mark, an achievement nobody wanted on their resume. On the other end of the spectrum, Luka Doncic (79.75 FP) and Victor Wembanyama (78.95 FP) nearly matched each other's best games to the decimal, a preview of the marquee showdown they'll have in Week 20's betting lines. Tyrese Maxey continues to lead the season in total fantasy points at 2972.10 despite missing time with a finger injury, while Nikola Jokic's 61.06 FPPG remains in a class of its own -- nearly 7 points per game clear of second-place Luka Doncic. The consistency data adds another layer: SGA owns the league's lowest CV at 24.9%, making him the most predictable star, while OG Anunoby's 57.6% CV marks him as the most volatile regular starter in the league -- a boom-or-bust wildcard on Nick's roster.

Around the NBA

  • Luka Doncic Drops 44 as Lakers Roll Past Pacers Without LeBron
    Luka Doncic put on one of the most spectacular individual performances of the season on March 6, scoring 44 points in the first three quarters of a 128-117 Lakers victory over the Indiana Pacers. He connected on seven three-pointers and has now averaged 30.9 points, 8.1 assists, and 7.7 rebounds per game since the All-Star break. For Nick, the 79.75 FP explosion was the single highest-scoring game of the fantasy week, and it came on a night LeBron James sat out -- meaning Doncic carried the load alone. With 2845.70 total FP on the season and a 54.72 FPPG clip, Luka continues to be the engine behind Nick's championship roster, and the post-All-Star-break surge suggests he's entering his highest gear at exactly the right time.
  • Wembanyama Powers Spurs Through Emotional 25-Point Comeback Against Clippers
    Victor Wembanyama had the kind of week that makes you recalibrate what a 22-year-old should be capable of. On Thursday, he went off for 38 points and 16 rebounds in a win over Detroit, then turned around Friday and helped the Spurs rally from a 25-point deficit to defeat the Clippers 116-112. After the final buzzer, Wembanyama collapsed into teammate De'Aaron Fox's arms, later telling ESPN he thought he was going to pass out from exhaustion in the first quarter. His 241.65 FP across 4 games (60.41 FPPG) earned him the POTW award for Garrett, who rode Wemby's dominance to a 4th consecutive victory. With Sabonis, Vucevic, and Curry all sidelined, Wembanyama has essentially become a one-man franchise for Garrett's fantasy squad -- and he's answered every call.
  • LeBron James Passes Kareem for Most Field Goals in NBA History
    LeBron James hit a turnaround baseline jump shot over Denver's Zeke Nnaji in the first quarter of Thursday's game to make his 15,838th career field goal, passing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the most in NBA history. The milestone came during a 120-113 Lakers loss in Denver -- the same game where Nikola Jokic (Hayden's cornerstone) clinched the win with a clutch bucket in the final 23 seconds. For Benton, the record is bittersweet: LeBron has been a productive fantasy asset when healthy, but he missed 2 games this week to injury and his 68.8% games played rate this season reflects the wear of year 23 in the league. With LeBron now just 5 games from Robert Parish's all-time games-played record, every healthy week from here on out matters for both NBA history and Benton's lineup stability.
  • Maxey Sprains Finger in Final Seconds Against Hawks -- Benton Braces for Impact
    Tyrese Maxey suffered a sprained right pinkie finger in the final seconds of Saturday's loss to the Hawks after colliding with teammate Adem Bona, and will miss at least two games. The injury is to the same finger that Maxey sprained last season, which hampered his shooting before prematurely ending his year. For Benton, the timing is brutal: Maxey leads the league in total fantasy points this season with 2972.10 FP and was having a career year at 48.72 FPPG. He's expected to return midweek in Week 20, but additional testing will determine the full treatment plan. In the meantime, Benton will need Cade Cunningham and Donovan Mitchell to carry the guard load in a Week 20 matchup against Hayden where Smaxey is a heavy -610 favorite.

Rumor Mill

Trade Ideas

  • Nick sends Chet Holmgren + Jalen Johnson to Garrett for Victor Wembanyama
    A consolidation play that would reshape both rosters. Nick, with the title already clinched, could afford to sacrifice depth for the generational upside of a 22-year-old Wembanyama (54.60 projected FPPG rest of season). Garrett gains two reliable starters -- Holmgren (35.05 FPPG) and Johnson (47.22 FPPG) -- to fill the holes left by Sabonis and Vucevic's season-ending injuries. Nick and Garrett have completed 6 trades all-time, including the Karl-Anthony Towns deal earlier this season in Week 7.
  • Hayden sends Nikola Jokic to Nick for Brandon Miller + Jalen Duren
    Selling high on the league's most dominant per-game player. With Hayden locked into 4th place at 4-15 and Jokic turning 31 next season, this is a dynasty-minded move -- trading peak production for two younger assets (Miller at 34.15 FPPG, Duren at 32.26 FPPG) who project as long-term keepers. Nick gets the ultimate win-now upgrade at center while Anthony Davis remains sidelined. Nick and Hayden have made 7 trades all-time, the second-most active partnership in the league behind Nick-Benton's 12.
  • Benton sends Desmond Bane to Garrett for Domantas Sabonis
    A straightforward swap that addresses clear positional needs. Benton gets a center who averaged 33.07 FPPG before his season-ending injury -- a stash-and-hold keeper play for next season. Garrett gets immediate forward help in Bane (32.47 FPPG this season, 100.0% games played). Benton and Garrett have made 4 deals all-time, and this low-risk swap fits both teams' timelines.
  • Benton sends Bam Adebayo to Garrett for Giannis Antetokounmpo
    A buy-low gamble on Giannis, who's been 17.4% below projection over the last 4 weeks and has played just 52.4% of games this season. Benton takes on the risk that Giannis's 47.91 projected FPPG rest of season materializes, while Garrett moves on from a player who's been a headache to manage and gets a reliable 36.98 FPPG center in Adebayo. The 24.5% season injury burden on Garrett's roster suggests he might be ready to consolidate for consistency.

Free Agent Targets

  • Rudy Gobert (C) -- Nick: 31.50 proj FPPG. Addresses Nick's injury-depleted center corps with Anthony Davis still weeks away. Eight games over the next two weeks makes the pickup even more appealing.
  • Grayson Allen (PG, SG, SF) -- Hayden: 31.19 proj FPPG. Fills Hayden's guard gaps with Jalen Williams and VJ Edgecombe sidelined. Versatile positional eligibility adds lineup flexibility.
  • Gui Santos (SF, PF) -- Garrett: 27.35 proj FPPG. Fills Garrett's forward weakness -- the position where he was outscored by 290 FP this week against Benton. Eight games over the next two weeks.
  • Alex Sarr (C) -- Garrett: 29.46 proj FPPG. Another option for Garrett's decimated center rotation with Sabonis and Vucevic done for the season and Giannis in and out of the lineup.
  • Keegan Murray (SF, PF) -- Hayden: 29.86 proj FPPG. Addresses Hayden's injury-depleted forward corps, where 27.10 FPPG this week was the league's worst positional output.

Hot Streaks

  • Isaiah Collier (Garrett): 33.4% above projection since joining Saboner -- consistently overperforming at 33.45 FPPG vs 25.07 projected. Sell high candidate -- actual 33.5 FPPG unlikely to sustain.
  • Donovan Clingan (Garrett): 25.8% above projection over the last 4 weeks, though cooling off at just 7.8% above over the last 14 days. Sell high candidate -- actual 41.3 FPPG unlikely to sustain. The sell window may be closing.
  • Kon Knueppel (Hayden): 24.5% above projection over the last 4 weeks on Big Nik Energy, 14.3% last 14 days -- cooling off. Elevated trade value -- producing 37.1 FPPG vs 29.8 projected.
  • OG Anunoby (Nick): 23.3% above projection since joining Luka my Balls -- consistently overperforming at 37.4 FPPG vs 30.3 projected. Elevated trade value, though Nick may want to keep the hot hand for his title defense.
  • Josh Giddey (Benton): 23.1% above projection over the last 4 weeks on Smaxey, 28.2% last 14 days -- trending up. Elevated trade value -- producing 42.9 FPPG vs 34.9 projected. May have more room to run.
  • Ryan Rollins (Nick): 21.5% above projection over the last 4 weeks on Luka my Balls, 14.0% last 14 days -- cooling off. Elevated trade value -- producing 35.2 FPPG vs 29.0 projected.

Slump Watch

  • Keyonte George (Hayden): 21.3% below projection over the last 4 weeks on Big Nik Energy, but just 2.3% below over the last 14 days -- showing clear improvement. Hold for now and monitor. Replacement: Reed Sheppard (32.90 FPPG) if the slump resumes.
  • Darius Garland (Nick): 17.7% below projection since joining Luka my Balls -- consistently underperforming at 30.11 FPPG vs 36.92 projected. No clear upgrade available on waivers -- hold for now despite the struggles.
  • Giannis Antetokounmpo (Garrett): 17.4% below projection over the last 4 weeks on Saboner -- consistently underperforming when healthy, compounded by a 52.4% games played rate. No clear replacement available, but with 47.91 projected FPPG rest of season, the ceiling is still elite if he can stay on the court.
  • Austin Reaves (Garrett): 18.8% below projection over the last 4 weeks on Saboner, 20.1% last 14 days -- consistently underperforming with no signs of turning the corner. No clear replacement available on waivers.
  • Amen Thompson (Nick): 17.8% below projection over the last 4 weeks on Luka my Balls, but just 9.2% below over the last 14 days -- showing improvement. Hold for now, as his 38.43 projected FPPG rest of season suggests the talent is there.
End of Newsletter
